title: Dairy Industry (Treatment and Disposal of Unfit Milk) Regulations 2007
title_en: Dairy Industry (Treatment and Disposal of Unfit Milk) Regulations 2007

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These regulations may be cited as the Dairy Industry (Treatment and disposal of unfit milk ) Regulations 2007 and shall come into effect on the date of publication.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply to all areas in which the Act applies and shall apply to Milk which is unfit for human consumption, or suspected of being unfit for human consumption or any milk forfeited by an Order of the Court under Section 25 of the Act or which in any other manner, found by an inspector to be distributed, sold or offered or exposed for sale, contrary to the provisions of the Act and Regulations made thereunder.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires: " Act " means the Dairy Industry Act No. 8 of 2004 . " Adulteration " means addition in milk of water or any other substances; or subtraction from milk any component and replacing it with other substances; " Board " means the Tanzania Dairy Board established under section 8 of the Act . " formal or official sample " means milk sample collected for analysis for the purpose of instituting legal proceedings; " Informal or unofficial milk sample " means a milk sample collected for the purpose of obtaining information and need not necessarily be analyzed; " Inspector " means the inspector appointed by the Board under the Act or regulations made under the Act ; " Analyst " means the analyst appointed by the Board tinder the regulations made under the Act ; " Post seizure sample " means a sample collected on Order of a Court in respect of milk under seizure; " sampling form " means a form for the collection of a milk sample as set out in the first Schedule to these Regulations: " seizure certificate " means a certificate as set out in the Third Schedule and Fourth Schedules to these regulations issued by inspector in respect of seized milk which is unfit for human consumption or which is suspected of being unfit for human consumption; " unfit milk " means milk which does not comply to the specifications of the Act or any regulations made thereunder; " voluntary condemnation certificate " means a certificate issued by an inspector in respect of seized milk which is unfit for human consumption; " Disposal " means to get rid of unfit milk in a manner which does not affect human health or the environment; " Milk " means raw milk.

provisions.title: Sampling and analysis of milk
provisions.content: (1) Sampling and analysis shall be done by inspectors and/or analysts appointed by the Board . (2) Sampling and analysis of milk shall be done in accordance with the Tanzania Standards procedures for milk. (3) Samples shall be taken at any stage for quality control and compliance to safety and quality standards as prescribed in the first Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Voluntary surrender of milk
provisions.content: (1) Any owner of milk or his agent upon being satisfied that his milk is unfit may voluntarily surrender it to an inspector for condemnation. (2) The inspector shall upon receiving and being satisfied that the milk surrendered under sub regulation (1) is unfit shall issue a voluntary surrender or condemnation certificate to the owner or his agent in the form set out in the Second Schedule to these regulations.

provisions.title: Alternative disposal of unfit milk
provisions.content: (1) An inspector may in his own motion or in agreement with the owner or the agent of the surrendered milk under regulation 5 of these regulations, direct that the milk be diverted for any other use other than human consumption provided that such use does not affect humans, animals or the environment. (2) An inspector who directs that the milk be diverted into alternative use as provided in sub regulation (1) above shall ensure that the process he has chosen is complete and effective.

provisions.title: Seizure of milk
provisions.content: (1) Where an inspector seizes any milk which is unfit for human consumption or which is suspected of being unfit for human consumption, he may:— (a) mark and remove the same from where it was found and inform the owner or his agent, of his intention to take legal measures and issue an appropriate seizure certificate as prescribed in the Third Schedule to the regulation; or (b) mark and put under custody and the care of the owner or his agent and in either case an inspector who seizes such milk shall issue an appropriate seizure certificate set out in the Third Schedule to these regulations.

provisions.title: Procedure for disposal of unfit milk
provisions.content: (1) An inspector who seizes milk which is unfit or which is suspected of being unfit in accordance with the Act or regulations made thereunder shall— (a) inform the owner of the milk or his agent, the exact reasons of the unfitness of such milk. (b) where the unfitness is such that the milk cannot be diverted to other uses, inform the owner of the milk or his agent, appropriate means of disposal to be carried out; and (c) where the owner of milk or his agent disagree with the opinion of an inspector in respect of the unfitness of the milk so seized and where such owner, or his agent, agree with the opinion of an inspector, the inspector shall ask the owner or his agent to indicate his agreement to that effect by signing a voluntary surrender or condemnation certificate in respect of that milk, as the case may be; and (d) where the opinion of an inspector is rejected by the owner or his agent, the inspector shall take the milk to the Court whose decision on the condition of the milk shall be binding on both parties. (2) If the Court confirms the opinion of the inspector in respect of unfit milk presented before it, the Inspector shall have the duty to: (a) dispose the milk in accordance with the order of the Court and be satisfied that the method of disposal used which ensures protection of human and animal health and the environment; (b) prepare a compulsory condemnation certificate in the form prescribed in the Fifth Schedule to these Regulations in respect of the unfit milk and give it to the owner or his agent. (3) In addition to any penalties provided under the Act and Regulations made thereunder, the owner or agent of the unfit milk in question shall bear the cost of disposing such milk

provisions.title: Methods of disposal of unfit milk
provisions.content: (1) Unfit milk shall be disposed of in such a manner that does not harm human and animal health and the environment. Methods of disposal shall be determined by the inspector among the following alternatives: (a) diversion to animal feeds in consultation with appropriate Authorities; (b) dumping onto a piece of non-productive land clear of watercourses, houses and dairy farms: dumping should be carried out on a different site followed by flushing with water to wash the milk off the leaves in order to minimize pasture damage and odour; (c) dumping onto an area that is to be cultivated; (d) dumping in drainage systems, oxidation ponds or any other appropriate system that are in compliance with Tanzania standards.

provisions.title: Offences and penalties
provisions.content: Any person who contravenes the provisions of these Regulations commits an offence and upon conviction shall be liable to a fine not exceeding fifty thousand shillings or to an imprisonment for a term not exceeding four months or to both such fine and imprisonment

provisions.title: Revocation
provisions.content: General Notice number 311 of 1968 is hereby revoked. First Schedule (Made under Regulation 4) Sampling, notification and receipt [Please note: The form has not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Second Schedule (Made under Regulation 5) Certificate of voluntary surrender of milk [Please note: The form has not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Third Schedule (Made under Regulation 7) Seizure certificate A [Please note: The form has not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Fourth Schedule (Made under Regulation 7) Seizure certificate B [Please note: The form has not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Fifth Schedule (Made under regulation 8) Certificate of condemnation of milk [Please note: The form has not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] History of this document 17 July 2020 amendment not yet applied Amended by Dairy Industry (Treatment and Disposal of Unfit Milk) (Amendment) Regulations, 2020 05 October 2007 this version Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania 40 Commenced 25 September 2007 Assented to Related documents Revokes National Dairy Board (Control of Marketing) (Mara Region) Order, 1968 Is amended by Dairy Industry (Treatment and Disposal of Unfit Milk) (Amendment) Regulations, 2020 More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
